key_insight: A vision-transformer model can classify narrow-band laryngeal endoscopy images as benign or malignant while pairing the prediction with segmentation-based explanations for clinicians.
clinical_relevance: This could support faster, more consistent screening of laryngeal lesions and help clinicians focus on suspicious pathological regions during decision-making.
method: Used a transformer with attention to analyze NBI endoscopy images.
Combined classification with MedSAM-based segmentation to highlight explainable lesion areas.
Designed the workflow to distinguish benign from malignant laryngeal lesions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong screening performance, with F1 of 82.72% and accuracy of 82.33%.
watch_item: Performance and explainability need validation on larger, multi-center datasets because endoscopy image quality, case mix, and interobserver variation can affect real-world reliability.
paper_title: Medical Imaging Fusing Vision Transformer: Laryngeal Cancer Screening with Explanation
